report:
Compared to the patient's previous examinations dated [date_1], [date_2], and [date_3].
A large parenchymal defect secondary to surgery is observed in the left temporopolar-temporomesial area. In the immediate posterior vicinity of the described surgical defect, wide T2-weighted hyperintense infiltration areas extending from the left parahippocampal area and left insular subcortical area towards the pulvinar thalami and peritrigonal area are observed, and compared to previous examinations, along with a decrease in the intensity of the T2-weighted hyperintensity observed in the described areas, generalized enlargement is also present in the left lateral ventricle (ex-vacuo enlargement). In the current examination, compared to the examination dated [date_3], a slight increase in size is observed in the T2-weighted hyperintense infiltration areas observed in the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle, which are progressively increasing. Additionally, there is also a slight increase in the size of T2-weighted hyperintensities extending from the left centrum semiovale and corpus callosum transcallosal fibers to the posterior right hemisphere frontal lobe.
Right lateral ventricle width is evaluated as stable, and a lacunar defect is observed anterior to the right insular subcortical area and minimal gliosis is observed in its periphery. T2-weighted hyperintense infiltration areas are observed in the right cerebellar peduncle and midbrain, right paramedian area of the pons, and right cerebral peduncle, and no significant difference is detected in the signal intensity and extension of the described areas compared to previous examinations. In the current examination, compared to previous examinations, there is an oval-shaped area measuring 1 cm in its widest diameter, extending from the midline level of the left sella to the globus pallidus, which is understood to have newly appeared, showing minimal peripheral contrast enhancement and hypointensity on ADC mapping (upgrade ?).
No difference was detected in other findings.
